independent_audit.freshness: synthetic same-run HOLD self-test; not an independent scientific audit

claim.text: A finite candidate can be tested while the Ratchet correctly refuses to advance on a flat distinction surface.
claim.obligation: Detect whether any intrinsic finite distinction potential exists before selecting a response.

weakening_coverage.detail: The weakening is registered, but a flat drive blocks admission before it can define a next-rung tooth.

controls.expected_effect: No object ontology is needed to detect a flat distinction potential.
controls.observed_effect: The root remained a constrained partial distinction record.

controls.expected_effect: Removing unused structure must not manufacture drive.
Relabeling must not create a gradient.
controls.observed_effect: The flat result survived label erasure.

controls.expected_effect: The decision must be computed rather than fixed to CLIMB.
Probe removal must not hide a positive registered gradient.
controls.observed_effect: No positive gradient existed before or after the probe mutation.

controls.expected_effect: Order commutation must not manufacture drive.
History erasure must not manufacture drive.
Resolution changes must be checked before interpreting a zero gradient.
controls.observed_effect: The zero persisted at every registered finite resolution.

controls.expected_effect: Changing presentation carrier must not manufacture drive.
controls.observed_effect: The rival finite presentation remained flat.

controls.expected_effect: No topology may be inferred from a HOLD.
The flat potential remains a property of one distinction surface.
controls.observed_effect: No independent entropy state was introduced.

controls.expected_effect: One flat record must not become a field law.
A changed obligation must require a new gradient measurement.
A held-out order must also be checked for hidden drive.
Freezing admissible distinction-changing updates must leave no authorized movement.
controls.observed_effect: The gradient was zero and no tooth was recorded.

controls.expected_effect: An already closed obligation must have zero residual potential.
An injected slope must be rejected as extrinsic.
controls.observed_effect: The receipt contains no injected drive.

controls.expected_effect: No candidate may be called a response when its obligation-relative delta is zero.
controls.observed_effect: The candidate delta was zero and no response was selected.

open_weaker_attacks: A changed obligation or resolution may expose a gradient.
A candidate outside this finite grammar may reveal a currently unmeasured distinction.

drive.functional: finite count of unresolved frozen distinction obligations
drive.orientation: positive magnitude would point toward obligation closure
drive.witness: Every registered finite candidate response leaves the frozen obligation-relative potential unchanged.

entropy_geometry.scope_note: The finite surface is flat relative to this frozen obligation; no entropy formula is installed.

transition.reason: No licensed distinction gradient exceeds tolerance, so there is no reason or direction for a Ratchet tooth.

reopen_triggers: A finer finite resolution reveals a nonzero distinction potential.
A new candidate produces a nonzero obligation-coupled response.
